LAW AND MOTION, 302, ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANTS ESTATE OF SHERWOOD J. ALLEN, DECEASED, KEYNOTE PROPERTIES, ASTRID LACITIS, ROBERT CAMOZZI, MARY WILLIAMS' MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT IS DENIED. THE ALLEN PARTIES HAD A DUTY TO TO DETERMINE THE FACTS RELATED TO THE DAMAGE TO THEIR PROPERTY AND THERE ARE NO FACTS JUSTIFYING THE DELAY IN SEEKING TO FILE THIS CLAIM AGAINST SUNSET. THE PROPOSED AMENDMENT COULD NOT RELATE BACK FOR IT INVOLVES INTENTIONAL CONDUCT FOR DAMAGE TO PROPERTY THAT IS NOT THE SUBJECT OF THE STATE FARM CASE. THE FACTS ALLEGED IN THE PROPOSED AMENDMENT DO NOT STATE FACTS TO SUPPORT A CLAIM FOR UNFAIR BUSINESS PRACTICES AND FALSE ADVERTISING. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR # 8600

LAW AND MOTION 302, ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: CROSS DEFENDANT SUNSET SCAVENGER COMPANY'S MOTION FOR DETERMINATION OF GOOD FAITH OF SETTLEMENT [CCP 877.6] IS GRANTED. THE SETTLEMENT MEETS THE REQUIREMENTS OF TECH-BILT. THERE ARE MATERIAL FACTUAL DISPUTES RELATED TO THE LIABILITY OF SUNSET AS WELL AS THE OWNERS AND PROPERTY MANAGERS GIVEN THE UNCERTAINTIES RELATED TO LIABILITY, THE SETTLEMENT IS A ROUGH APPROXIMATION OF PLAINTIFF'S POTENTIAL RECOVERY AND SUNSET'S PROPORTIONATE SHARE, RECOGNIZING THAT SUNSET SHOULD PAY LESS IN SETTLEMENT. THE MOTION IS GRANTED BASED ON REPRESENTATION OF STATE FARM AND SUNSET THAT THE WORK OF THE JOINT EXPERT, JAMES MCMULLEN, WILL NOT BE DISCLOSED, AND THAT ANY JOINT WORK PRODUCT MATERIALS POSSESSED BY SUNSET SHALL NOT BE DISCLOSED. ALLEN'S OBJECTIONS TO THE EVIDENCE IS OVERRULED. TO EVALUATE TECH BILT, IT IS NECESSARY FOR THE COURT TO EVALUATE THE FACTS THAT SUPPORT THE PARTIES STATE OF MIND. THE COURT IS NOT DETERMING LIABILITY AND THE FACTS AND ARGUMENTS OF COUNSEL ONLY GO TO ASSESSING THE GOOD FAITH OF THE SETTLEMENT. THE REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE DOES NOT COMPLY WITH RULES OF COURT 3.113(M) AND 3.1306(C) AND FOR THAT REASON THE REQUEST IS DENIED. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR # 8600

ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING.
CROSS DEFENDANT SUNSET SCAVENGER COMPANY'S Notice Of Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Of Settlement [Ccp 877.6] IS GRANTED. THE SETTLEMENT MEETS THE REQUIREMENTS OF TECH-BILT. THERE ARE MATERIAL FACTUAL DISPUTES RELATED TO THE LIABILITY OF SUNSET AS WELL AS THE OWNERS AND PROPERTY MANAGERS GIVEN THE UNCERTAINTIES RELATED TO LIABILITY, THE SETTLEMENT IS A ROUGH APPROXIMATION OF PLAINTIFF'S POTENTIAL RECOVERY AND SUNSET'S PROPORTIONATE SHARE, RECOGNIZING THAT SUNSET SHOULD PAY LESS IN SETTLEMENT. THE COURT IS NOT DETERMING LIABILITY AND THE FACTS AND ARGUMENTS OF COUNSEL ONLY GO TO ASSESSING THE GOOD FAITH OF THE SETTLEMENT.
